title: Brachiaria eminii
taxonomy: Brachiaria eminii is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Brachiaria, which comprises numerous species of grasses found in tropical and subtropical regions. Brachiaria eminii is native to tropical Africa and has been introduced to other parts of the world, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. The species is known for its ability to thrive in a variety of environments, including wetlands and upland areas. As a member of the Poaceae family, Brachiaria eminii is a monocotyledonous plant with characteristic features such as parallel veins and a single cotyledon in its seedling stage.
